message:
feat(cli): gate CLI surface behind ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES

Wraps the entire CLI public API and implementation with
#if ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES guards so the CLI family is
hidden from browser builds. Updates the capability matrix from
"gap" to "partial" for CLI/browser compatibility.

Adds unit tests for CLI helpers (parse_output_format, output_format_str,
config_init) that are also gated behind the runtime surface check.

Closes bd-4bi1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3vt.7","title":"Evaluate optional alpha tracks (seqlock metadata and EBR/hazard reclamation) with fallback parity checks","description":"Background:
Plan includes optional advanced tracks for parallel profile performance; adoption requires proof and rollback.

Subtasks:
- Prototype seqlock metadata path and evaluate correctness/performance tradeoffs.
- Prototype EBR or hazard-based reclamation path for ownership-sensitive structures.
- Validate fallback paths and parity behavior against baseline locking approach.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each track has explicit go/no-go decision with evidence.
- Fallback remains implemented and verified.
- No semantic drift is introduced by adoption.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.323728605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:44:37.416342272Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:44:37.416319800Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","parallel","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.8","title":"Prototype time-virtualization layer for jitter and clock-anomaly replay","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 highlights time virtualization as a lever for reproducing jitter and clock anomalies deterministically.

Subtasks:
- Define virtual-time injection API boundaries and scheduling interactions.
- Extend scenario DSL with clock anomaly events (jitter, stall, jump).
- Build replay tests showing deterministic reproduction of anomaly scenarios.
- Evaluate operational overhead and integration risks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Provides clear go/no-go recommendation with benchmarked overhead.
- Anomaly scenarios reproduce deterministically across repeated runs.
- Fallback to physical-time mode remains intact and documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.503981512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T07:09:49.925389641Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T07:09:49.925365135Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","determinism","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.9","title":"Define hot-reload-safe config boundaries and semantic freeze points","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 proposes hot-reload-safe config boundaries so runtime tuning does not mutate semantics mid-flight.

Subtasks:
- Classify config fields as hot-reload-safe, freeze-at-start, or restart-required.
- Define semantic freeze points where updates are applied atomically.
- Add tests for mid-run update safety and rollback on invalid updates.
- Document operator workflow for safe live tuning.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config reload behavior is deterministic and policy-driven.
- Unsafe mid-run updates are rejected with explicit diagnostics.
- Replay and semantic digest guarantees remain intact.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.586821141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:14:59.210085015Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:14:59.210057855Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced"]}
+{"id":"bd-4bi1","title":"Hide public CLI family from browser builds and prove it with focused tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-24T19:19:45.333628096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T19:23:24.232808092Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T19:23:24.232389711Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-56t","title":"EPIC: Release, Evidence, and Operational Readiness","description":"Background:
A high-assurance runtime needs repeatable release mechanics plus evidence bundles for audits and regulated contexts.

Goal:
Define release automation, artifact integrity checks, and evidence packaging that map implementation behavior to documented guarantees.

Outputs:
- release runbooks
- signed/checksummed artifacts
- certification-ready evidence skeletons

Why this serves users:
- production confidence for operators
- reduced friction for safety-critical adoption.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.559479025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T06:06:02.706966537Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T06:06:02.706944196Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","ops","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.1","title":"Implement release automation and integrity artifacts for asx targets","description":"Background:
Releases must be repeatable and verifiable across supported targets.

Subtasks:
- Define build and packaging pipeline for target artifacts.
- Emit checksum and signature bundles for each release asset.
- Define asset naming/versioning conventions and provenance metadata.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Release pipeline is reproducible and documented.
- Integrity metadata is generated automatically.
- Release artifacts map cleanly to CI evidence and version tags.","notes":"Implemented in this session: tools/ci/run_release_artifacts.sh (deterministic tar.xz + sha256 + sigstore bundle + provenance metadata for binary/source), .github/workflows/release.yml (tag-triggered v* automation + validation + publish), Makefile release-artifacts target, docs/QUALITY_GATES.md gate entry, README release artifact contract. Local validation: bash -n script OK; source and linux-x86_64 artifact generation OK. rch suite: format-check/lint/build pass; test/conformance hit remote permission issues in worker context, unrelated to this release-flow code path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyCliff","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:14.113965360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:34.482371111Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:34.482350553Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","automation","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.1","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.1","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.2","title":"Create release verification and rollback runbook","description":"Background:
Operational users need deterministic post-release validation and safe rollback procedures.

Subtasks:
- Define verification checklist for artifact presence, signatures, and smoke tests.
- Require release-candidate e2e runs (kernel lifecycle, codec equivalence, profile parity, restart continuity, vertical HFT/automotive/router lanes) with archived manifests.
- Define rollback triggers and rollback procedure by release class.
- Define incident-response references for replay/conformance validation post-deploy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runbook enables repeatable release validation by any maintainer.
- Rollback path is fast and tested in at least one dry run.
- Includes profile-specific smoke verification guidance.
- Release approval requires complete structured logs and artifact manifests from required e2e runs.","notes":"Implemented release verification/rollback runbook in docs/DEPLOYMENT_HARDENING.md with: release classes, deterministic pre-publish checklist, required artifact/log manifest set, profile-specific smoke requirements, rollback triggers, rollback procedures by class, and post-deploy replay/conformance references. Added README link to runbook. Performed dry-run validation on 2026-03-01 using tools/ci/run_release_artifacts.sh for source+binary targets and checksum verification in build/release/dist.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyCliff","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:14.218832958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:18:26.673220203Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:18:26.673202419Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release","runbook"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-56t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-yx9r","title":"Reopened full-crate parity closure against /dp/asupersync export surface","description":"The previously closed roadmap mostly covered kernel and selective deferred surfaces, but /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs still exports many major module families that are not materially present in the ANSI C tree: config, decoding, epoch, evidence, evidence_sink, gen_server, http, io, link, migration, monitor, observability, obligation, record, remote, service, session, spork, transport, types, web, plus feature/platform modules such as cli, database, tls, grpc, messaging, and server. This epic reopens parity planning at the crate level instead of stopping at kernel semantics.","design":"Use /dp/asupersync/README.md, /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs, the upstream module tree, and the local deferred-surface docs as the primary evidence base. The graph should separate: (1) truth-maintenance tasks that correct the parity narrative, (2) foundation tracks needed before protocol/application stacks, and (3) end-state acceptance/gating work that proves crate-level parity or explicitly records remaining deliberate exclusions. Every implementation track must carry unit tests, integration/conformance fixtures where possible, and logged e2e or smoke artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"A. The reopened backlog explicitly covers every major Rust export family that remains absent or only stubbed in ANSI C.\nB. The graph distinguishes foundational blockers from higher-surface protocol/application work.\nC. Each child issue stores enough background and verification detail that the original markdown plans are unnecessary.\nD. Final acceptance requires crate-level parity evidence rather than kernel-only success criteria.","notes":"Primary upstream evidence: /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs, /dp/asupersync/README.md, and upstream module directories such as src/http, src/web, src/grpc, src/service, src/transport, src/distributed, src/database, src/messaging, src/types, src/record, src/obligation, src/lab, src/trace, and src/runtime/reactor. Local evidence that the old closure was too narrow: docs/DEFERRED_SURFACES.md, docs/DEFERRED_SURFACE_REGISTER.md, and docs/SELECTIVE_SURFACE_PORTING_QUEUE.md.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T20:38:15.573296808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:08:49.502027442Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:08:49.501535602Z","close_reason":"Full-crate parity closure COMPLETE. All 329 beads closed. Acceptance gate passes: 134 unit tests, 20 e2e families, 33 codec-equivalence records, profile-parity verified. 43+ subsystems implemented across core, runtime, channel, sync, actor, time, bytes, security, net (TCP/UDP/Unix/TLS/WS/QUIC/HTTP/web/gRPC/db/messaging/distributed), service, transport, remote, spork, and more.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","core-parity","crate-parity","reopened","upstream-gap"]}

@@ -268,10 +268,10 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections, and browser builds now expose two compile-time browser subprofiles via `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 2` with default-extended semantics; remaining debt is deeper upstream feature granularity beyond the new minimal/extended split |
-| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, and `include/asx/fs/fs.h`, `include/asx/process/process.h`, `include/asx/signal/signal.h`, `include/asx/app/app.h`, `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
+| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `cli`, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, and `include/asx/fs/fs.h`, `include/asx/process/process.h`, `include/asx/signal/signal.h`, `include/asx/app/app.h`, `include/asx/cli/cli.h`, `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
 | `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Native I/O blocked under browser boundary and compile-time capability macros | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_IO_DRIVER` is denied in browser mode and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER == 0`, but there is still no separate browser-IO feature flag or profile-minimal compile check |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Browser minimal-vs-extended subprofiles now gate the public trace family | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `trace`/`telemetry`/`replay`/`lab`/`regression_localize`/`tracing_compat` from the umbrella in minimal browser builds, and the direct headers now self-gate those families unless internal implementation files opt in via `ASX_INTERNAL_TRACE_FAMILY_ACCESS`; remaining debt is broader upstream feature parity beyond this trace-family split |
-| `cli` unsupported on browser builds | No `cli` module in C | `gap` | The C port currently lacks a public CLI family entirely, so there is no feature-gated fail-closed browser rule to enforce |
+| `cli` unsupported on browser builds | Public CLI family exists and is now browser-hidden | `partial` | The C tree ships `include/asx/cli/cli.h` and `src/cli/cli.c`, and browser builds now hide that family both from `include/asx/asx.h` and from direct `include/asx/cli/cli.h` inclusion via `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES`; remaining debt is broader compile-time/browser feature-matrix coverage beyond the CLI surface itself |
 | `io-uring` unsupported on browser builds | No io_uring feature surface in C | `mapped-by-absence` | No equivalent feature exists yet; once native reactor features expand, browser incompatibility must become explicit |
 | `tls`, `tls-native-roots`, `tls-webpki-roots` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped TLS family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_TLS` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning TLS entry points, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, but there is still no sub-feature split for trust-root variants |
 | `sqlite`, `postgres`, `mysql` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped database family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_DATABASE` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning database entry points, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, but there is still no backend-specific compile-time split |
